metadata.title: Bitcoin Developer Quantum Urgency Map

scoring_rubric.1: No known view - no verifiable public statements found
scoring_rubric.2: Aware, dismissive - acknowledges QC but considers it non-issue in foreseeable timeframe
scoring_rubric.3: Aware, cautious - acknowledges risk, believes research warranted but upgrade not yet urgent
scoring_rubric.4: Concerned, proactive - believes Bitcoin should begin concrete preparation now
scoring_rubric.5: Urgent, advocating - actively pushing for imminent PQC protocol changes

metadata.critical_context: Google Quantum AI (March 31, 2026): <500k physical qubits break ECDSA-256 in ~9 min — inside Bitcoin's block time. 20x easier than 2019 estimates. ~37% of BTC supply in quantum-vulnerable P2PK addresses. Zero public upgrade roadmap. Zero top developer response within 48 hours of the Google paper. BIP-360 merged but no activation timeline. Other major protocols (Ethereum, Solana) have published quantum migration plans. Bitcoin has not.
metadata.key_insight: Bitcoin is sleepwalking into a quantum crisis. Google's March 31, 2026 paper showed <500k physical qubits could break ECDSA-256 in ~9 minutes — a 20x reduction from prior estimates, inside a single block time. ~37% of Bitcoin's supply sits in addresses with exposed public keys. Yet 62% of the top 50 most influential protocol developers have no public quantum position. The dismissive camp outnumbers the urgent camp nearly 3-to-1. No current Core maintainer scores above a 2. Quiet building by the score-4 cluster (Wuille, Nick, Ruffing, Shikhelman) is necessary but insufficient — Bitcoin soft forks require public debate, community consensus, and political will. None of that is happening. BIP-360 exists but has no activation timeline. Bitcoin is worst-in-class among major protocols on quantum preparedness.
metadata.x_source_note: X/Twitter post dates confirmed via snowflake ID decoding. Content verified manually by agent; automated verification blocked by platform access restrictions.

update_history.change: 50 ranked developers imported from Issue #30 research
score remains 1 — documented null result April 2026
score 3→4 — two-step PQ upgrade path advocacy
score 1→3 — HRF quantum threat report + PQ research funding
pq_work_volume 1→2 — PQ HD wallet rescue prototype
new entry — score 2 (aware, dismissive of urgency-based action); JoinMarket creator; bitcoin-dev Algorithm Agility thread Apr 2026
new entry — score 4 (Proactive), pq_work_volume 2 — BIP Draft Witness v3 ML-DSA-65 + reference impl (btcpqresearch/bip-pq-witness-v3), DelvingBitcoin Apr 16 2026
added as notable — score 4 (Proactive), pq_work_volume 1; EUROCRYPT 2026 paper IACR 2026/280, 44% logical-qubit reduction for prime-curve ECDLP (P-256: 1,193 qubits), inherited by secp256k1

developers.name: Pieter Wuille
developers.summary: Pro-confiscation of QC-vulnerable coins; embedded PQ future-proofing in Taproot/BIP-324 design; treats threat as real but conditional ('big if')

developers.name: Wladimir J. van der Laan
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; retired from Bitcoin development 2022

developers.name: Gregory Maxwell
developers.summary: Discussed PQ signatures on mailing list Dec 2025 - acknowledges the topic but no urgency stance. Retired from active development ~2018. Historical rank only.

developers.name: Anthony Towns
developers.summary: No standalone quantum position; co-authored Taproot but no personal advocacy

developers.name: Michael Ford
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; focused on build systems and release management

developers.name: Ava Chow
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; focused on wallet infrastructure

developers.name: Jonas Nick
developers.summary: Most technically active PQ researcher; building SHRINCS (324-byte PQ sigs) and SHRIMPS (multi-device); BIP-360 reviewer; published ePrint paper; presenting at OPNEXT Apr 2026

developers.name: Andrew Poelstra
developers.summary: Engaged with Lamport/quantum-adjacent work; no direct quantum urgency advocacy

developers.name: Gloria Zhao
developers.summary: 'That concern is a bit more interesting, on the timescale of 30 to 50 years.' Aware but considers it very distant.

developers.name: Ryan Ofsky
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; focused on multiprocess architecture

developers.name: TheCharlatan
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; focused on libbitcoinkernel

developers.name: Hennadii Stepanov
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; focused on build system/GUI

developers.name: Suhas Daftuar
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; focused on P2P relay and mempool

developers.name: Alex Morcos
developers.summary: No personal statements; org published quantum report (Shikhelman May 2025)

developers.name: Luke Dashjr
developers.summary: 'Quantum isn't a real threat. Bitcoin has much bigger problems to address.' Outright dismissal.

developers.name: Mark Erhardt (Murch)
developers.summary: '...make fun of me if we see a CRQC in less than 20 years.' Dismissive with humor.

developers.name: Ethan Heilman
developers.summary: Most urgent advocate; co-author BIP-360 and BIP-347; 'near future' urgency; proposed STARK compression; estimates 7-year migration timeline

developers.name: Hunter Beast
developers.summary: Original BIP-360 champion; multiple BIP iterations since 2024; 'ready in event of quantum emergency'

developers.name: Greg Sanders
developers.summary: 'Proof will be in the pudding and I'll change my tune. Until then skepticism reigns.'

developers.name: Eric Lombrozo
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; less active recently

developers.name: Johnson Lau
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; less active recently

developers.name: Tim Ruffing
developers.summary: 2018 commit-reveal proposal for QC transition; 2025 paper proving Taproot PQ-secure as commitment scheme; methodical preparation without panic

developers.name: Antoine Poinsot
developers.summary: 'The real material threat...is the perception of an imminent existential quantum threat by important stakeholders.' FUD is more dangerous than actual risk.

developers.name: Jon Atack
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; BIP editor

developers.name: Ruben Somsen
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk; BIP editor

developers.name: Olaoluwa Osuntokun
developers.summary: Engaged with PQ HD wallet derivation on bitcoin-dev (Dec 2025): DilithiumRK, Falcon, SPHINCS+. On Apr 8, 2026 posted zk-STARK proof of BIP-32 seed knowledge enabling BIP-86 Taproot wallet recovery to bitcoin-dev mailing list. Proof generation ~55s on consumer MacBook, verification <2s, ~1.7 MB proof size (per CoinDesk). First executable code for post-quantum Taproot wallet recovery without exposing the seed. Research-engaged, building working tools, not advocating for urgency.

developers.name: Bryan Bishop (kanzure)
developers.summary: No quantum statements found across gnusha.org and diyhpl.us transcript archives

developers.name: Fabian Jahr
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: John Newbery
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Mike Schmidt
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Steve Lee
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Sebastian Falbesoner
developers.summary: Working on FROST (quantum-tangential) but no quantum position statements

developers.name: Dave Harding
developers.summary: Optech covers quantum objectively (BIP-360, Chaincode report, SHRINCS/SLH-DSA); no personal advocacy position

developers.name: Niklas Gögge
developers.summary: Focuses on Bitcoin Core security through full-system fuzzing and P2P infrastructure hardening. Developer of the Fuzzamoto framework and serves as a mentor for security-focused Brink interns. No public statements found regarding quantum risk or post-quantum cryptography migration.

developers.name: Marco Falke
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Peter Todd
developers.summary: 'No-one is even close... the actual capabilities are laughable.' Also frames PQ push as potential NSA backdoor.

developers.name: Antoine Riard
developers.summary: Cautious: wants better Shor's threat modeling per QC architecture; engaged reviewer but not pushing urgency; proposed practical defenses

developers.name: Isabel Foxen Duke
developers.summary: BIP-360 co-author (Dec 2025 rewrite); actively building quantum resistance path for Bitcoin

developers.name: Sjors Provoost
developers.summary: 'I don't think that in practice we can deploy a PCQ scheme without at the same time making a decision with regards to burn vs free-for-all.' Engaged in policy not just technical.

developers.name: Clara Shikhelman
developers.summary: Authored Chaincode quantum report (May 2025): 20-50% of BTC potentially vulnerable; 7-year comprehensive plan recommended; leading institutional research effort

developers.name: Alex Gladstein
developers.summary: Published comprehensive HRF report "The Quantum Threat to Bitcoin" (Oct 31, 2025) warning 6.51M BTC vulnerable within 5 years. Supports BIP-360 as quantum-resistant framework. HRF Bitcoin Development Fund to sponsor PQ research, testnets, and migration tooling. Addressed burn-or-steal dilemma for vulnerable coins.

developers.name: Adam Jonas
developers.summary: Chaincode Labs under Jonas published the foundational 2025 study estimating 20-50% BTC supply vulnerability. Jonas now advocates for BIP-360 (P2QRH) as a critical multi-year 'protection layer' for the network.

developers.name: Neha Narula
developers.summary: Published compound probability framework April 3, 2026: P(CRQC by 2030) × P(Bitcoin fails to upgrade) ≥5% existential risk. Calls for prioritizing PQ design now. First non-BIP-360-author, non-Blockstream researcher to cross into the proactive camp.

developers.name: Karl-Johan Alm
developers.summary: Distinguished hash-protected vs exposed-pubkey quantum risk in bitcoin-dev thread. Argued Taproot naked pubkeys give attackers unlimited time vs hashed keys where attacker must race transaction confirmation. Cautious position: recognizes real quantum threat vector in P2TR design.

developers.name: Carla Kirk-Cohen
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Mark Friedenbach
developers.summary: Opposed Taproot activation explicitly over quantum risk. Argued P2TR exposes public keys on-chain, making ~6.25M BTC (1/3 of supply) immediately vulnerable to quantum theft. Called Taproot activation without PQ mitigation irresponsible.

developers.name: Lisa Neigut
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Ben Price
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: 0xB10C
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Matias Furszyfer
developers.summary: No public statements on quantum risk

developers.name: Matt Corallo
developers.summary: Proposed OP_HASHBASEDSIG (SPHINCS+) on bitcoin-dev, then laid out a concrete two-step quantum upgrade path on Unchained podcast (Feb 22, 2026): enable PQ key commitments via soft fork first, disable legacy spend paths later. Actively pushing back on the no-one-is-building narrative, noting PQ discussion is now 30-40% of mailing list posts.

developers.name: Adam Back
developers.summary: Shifted from dismissive ('20-40 years, ridiculously early') to active preparation (Apr 8 CoinDesk): 'prepare Bitcoin and give people a decade to migrate keys to quantum-ready format.' Revealed 20-person Blockstream PQ research team building on Liquid as proving ground. NIST standards adoption underway. No longer dismissing -- actively building.

developers.name: John Martinis
developers.role: Nobel Laureate (2025 Physics), White House PCAST member
developers.summary: Called Bitcoin's elliptic curve cryptography "the smaller, easier algorithms. The low-hanging fruit" for quantum attacks (Benzinga, April 7 2026). Stated "Bitcoin is a little bit different, which is why people should be thinking about this right now" and "the crypto community has to plan for this. It's a serious issue." Suggested a 5-10 year window. Led Google quantum supremacy demonstration (Sycamore, 2019). Shared 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics. Joined White House PCAST March 26 2026. CTO and co-founder of Qolab.

developers.name: Jameson Lopp
developers.summary: Authored BIP-361 (PR #1895): ties legacy-signature sunset rules to supply inflation guardrails. Explicitly frames quantum migration as both cryptographic and monetary policy question. Active BIP contributor since July 2025.

developers.name: Avihu Levy
developers.summary: Published Quantum Safe Bitcoin (QSB) on Apr 9, 2026 -- first scheme enabling quantum-resistant Bitcoin transactions without any protocol changes. Uses hash-based proofs (RIPEMD-160 grinding) instead of ECDSA, achieving 118-bit pre-image resistance against quantum attack. Cost: $75-200 GPU compute per transaction. No soft fork, no BIP required. Also co-authored BIP-360.

developers.name: Adam Gibson (waxwing / AdamISZ)
developers.summary: Opposes mandatory coin-freezing on principled grounds. In the Algorithm Agility bitcoin-dev thread (Apr 2026): ‘We won’t know for sure when the world shifts from outputs are safe to claimable by anyone.’ Argues Bitcoin’s value derives from immutable property rights — ‘if you locked a utxo with a certain ruleset in the past, that ruleset will still be active.’ Acknowledges quantum uncertainty but maintains principles outweigh safety-based arguments for coin freezing. Advocates opt-in PQ solutions (P2MR) only. Creator of JoinMarket, Bitcoin privacy researcher.

developers.name: Echelon Labs Research
developers.summary: Published BIP Draft ‘Witness Version 3: ML-DSA-65 Post-Quantum Key-Path Spending’ on DelvingBitcoin on Apr 16, 2026, with a full working reference implementation (btcpqresearch/bip-pq-witness-v3) applying cleanly against bitcoin/bitcoin at commit c97ac44c. Proposes OP_3 + bech32m bc1r outputs, 5,261-byte witness stacks (3,309-byte sig + 1,952-byte pubkey), PQSighash tagged hash (epoch 1, ext_flag 3), SCRIPT_VERIFY_WITNESS_V3 flag, deployable as BIP 9 soft fork. Offered as a candidate PQ scheme for BIP-361 Phase B migration.

developers.name: Chevignard, Fouque, Schrottenloher (INRIA)
developers.summary: EUROCRYPT 2026 paper (IACR ePrint 2026/280) 'Reducing the Number of Qubits in Quantum ECDLP' presents prime-curve ECDLP quantum circuits achieving 3.12n + o(n) qubits versus 5n + o(n) prior (Häner 2020). For P-256: 1,193 logical qubits — a 44% reduction. Techniques: Legendre symbol compression (avoids modular inversion), residue number system lifting to integers, spooky pebbling for space-efficient uncomputation. P-256 now breaks with 42% fewer logical qubits than RSA-3072. Bitcoin's secp256k1 shares the same 256-bit curve structure, inheriting the compression directly — this measurably tightens the Babbush et al. <500K physical-qubit ECDLP-256 budget.
